The upper part shows the experimental setup with multi-RE SLP cells that was exclusively used for validation. In the lower part, the elements implemented on the BMS microcontroller are depicted. A dedicated integrated circuit used to measure the full-cell voltage is shown at the top right.

Caption: OCPs of negative and positive electrode determined by GITT in multi-RE cells. Identification of stoichiometric ranges using GITT-OCP data from coin cell measurements and their use for extrapolation outside the full cell (FC) utilization window.

Optimization procedure to determine exchange current densities and solid diffusivity values as a function of the lithiation degree from GITT in multi-RE cells. Sequential parameter variation was performed to minimize the error between the simulated and measured voltage response for each of the 1% SoC steps. Using the spline interpolation shown, the results were implemented in the model.

Caption: Simplified illustration showing the vectorized implementation of the calculation of all partial derivatives of the model equations g i with respect to the state variables. Caption: Validation of the suitability of the anode surface potential estimate (a, b) to indicate the onset threshold of lithium plating at current rates ranging from 0.5C to 2C. The measured negative half-cell potentials (b, c), DCVAs of the negative half-cell potentials (e, f) and their derivatives (g, h) are shown for the two cell types differing only by the electrolyte used. The results are shown for an arbitrarily selected RE located inside the active area. EIS (i, j) performed during charging at full-cell level is used as a second method to identify the onset of lithium plating. Finally, the current profiles during CV phases can also be used as an indirect method. The vertical dashed lines indicate the point at which lithium plating is predicted/detected by the respective method. Note that EIS was not performed during 2C charging for cell type 2.

Caption: Demonstration of the closed-loop charging current controller with CC, CAP, and CV phases on the microcontroller for 1C (a), 1.5C (b), and 2C (c).
